name: Tower
description_short: Tower Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving Maryland, DC, and Virginia with deposit accounts, home loans, and digital banking services for 70 years.
description_long:
Tower Federal Credit Union has operated for 70 years as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution serving the Maryland, DC, and Virginia region. The credit union is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional banks with a community-focused mission. The organization emphasizes member value through competitive rates, lower fees, and personalized service across multiple delivery channels.

Tower offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share savings accounts, share certificates (CDs), checking accounts, home loans (mortgages, home equity loans, home equity lines of credit), personal loans, credit cards, and digital banking services. Members can access accounts through online banking, a mobile app with mobile deposit capabilities, digital wallets for smartphone payments, and in-person at branch locations. The credit union also provides identity theft protection through ID Smart Shield and a virtual assistant named Tori available 24/7 via chat.

Tower distinguishes itself through specific quantified member benefits: members earned deposit rates twice the average of Maryland banks, saved $5.1 million collectively in fees compared to traditional banks, and saved $30.3 million on home loans. The credit union maintains active community involvement, including a sponsorship of the Baltimore Orioles' Bird Bath and a "Pennies for Change" program that has donated over $1.2 million to support Veterans and military families. The organization emphasizes accessibility with extended hours (Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 9am-2pm) and multiple contact methods including phone, email, live chat, and appointment scheduling.

Tower Federal Credit Union is a legitimate, well-established financial institution appropriate for members seeking competitive rates and member-owned governance. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—membership eligibility requires connection to Maryland, DC, or Virginia (details on membership requirements are not provided on the website). As a credit union rather than a bank, it may have fewer branch locations than national banks, though digital banking options mitigate this concern for most consumers.
services: Share savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Checking accounts with digital banking access
Home equity loans and home equity lines of credit
Credit cards with Pennies for Change donation feature
Mobile banking app with mobile check deposit
Digital wallet integration for smartphone payments
Money transfer between Tower accounts and external institutions
ID Smart Shield identity theft protection service
pros: Deposit rates advertised as twice the average of Maryland banks, providing higher savings account returns
Members saved $5.1 million in fees collectively, indicating significantly lower fee structures than competitor banks
Members saved $30.3 million on mortgages and home equity products, showing competitive home lending rates
24/7 virtual assistant (Tori) available via chat for member support without waiting for business hours
Mobile deposit capability allows check deposits anytime, anywhere via smartphone app
Pennies for Change program donated over $1.2 million to Veterans and military families, demonstrating community commitment
Extended member service center hours including Saturday availability (9am-2pm EST)
cons: Geographic limitation: appears to serve only Maryland, DC, and Virginia members; national expansion not evident
Website does not clearly specify membership eligibility requirements or how to qualify for membership
Limited information about loan product details, rates, or terms available on the homepage
No mention of business accounts or services for small business members
Mobile app functionality mentioned but not downloadable or reviewable directly from website
best_for: Residents of Maryland, DC, and Virginia seeking higher deposit rates than traditional banks
Homeowners looking for competitive mortgage and home equity loan rates in the Mid-Atlantic region
Military members and Veterans interested in supporting community programs through everyday banking
Consumers prioritizing lower fees and member-owned governance over national bank convenience
diagnosis: Tower Federal Credit Union is best for Maryland, DC, and Virginia residents seeking member-owned banking with demonstrably higher savings rates and lower fees than traditional banks. The primary caveat is geographic restriction to the Mid-Atlantic region; prospective members must verify eligibility and confirm the credit union serves their area before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Tower — Credit Union in LAUREL, MD | CreditDoc
meta_description: Tower — NCUA-insured credit union in LAUREL, MD. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
